docket_entries.description: REPLY IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT SWANSON, MCNAMARA & HALLER MCNAMARA, MARY GUGELMANN, AUGUST

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SWANSON, MCNAMARA & HALLER; MARY MCNAMARA, AND, AUGUST GUGELMANN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AND MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED, BOTH WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SWANSON, MCNAMARA & HALLER, MARY MCNAMARA, AND, AUGUST GUGELMANN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH AND PLEAD ACTUAL INNOCENCE IN A POST CONVICTION PROCEEDING. PLAINTIFF'S ACTUAL INNOCENCE OF THE UNDERLYING CRIMINAL CHARGES IS A NECESSARY ELEMENT OF THE MALPRACTICE CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE REDANTE VS. YOCKELSON (2003) 112 CAL.APP. 4TH 1351, 1357). REQUEST FOR STAY IS DENIED. CLAIM DOES NOT ACCRUE UNTIL PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHED HIS ACTUAL INNOCENCE. NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY OF CURING DEFECT BY AMENDMENT. (DEFENSE COUNSEL FAXED PLAINTIFF THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING THE PREVIOUS DAY; AND, DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER AND SEND PLAINTIFF NOTICE OF TODAY'S RULING BY THE COURT.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike is off calendar as moot in view of ruling on demurrer. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SWANSON, MCNAMARA & HALLER, MARY MCNAMARA, and, AUGUST GUGELMANN DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: James A. Murphy, Esq. of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415) 788-1900 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to establish and plead actual innocence in a post conviction proceeding. Plaintiff's actual innocence of the underlying criminal charges is a necessary element of the malpractice cause of action. (See Redante vs. Yockelson (2003) 112 Cal.App. 4th 1351, 1357). Request for stay is denied. Claim does not accrue until plaintiff established his actual innocence. No reasonable possibility of curing defect by amendment. (Defense counsel faxed Plaintiff the Court's tentative ruling the previous day; and, Defense counsel will prepare order and send Plaintiff notice of today's ruling by the Court.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Continued To Aug-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SWANSON, MCNAMARA & HALLER; MARY MCNAMARA, and, AUGUST GUGELMANN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Bryan L. P. Saalfeld, of Murphy Pearson Bradley & Feeney (415) 788-1900, appearing for Defendants and moving party. There are no other appearances. Counsel states he did give notice to Plaintiff by mail of his intention to argue. Defendant is contesting the leave to amend portion of the ruling. After hearing argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained and motion to strike is granted, both with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to plead actual innocence, identify his injury, show a proximate causal connection, or allege facts sufficient to support punitive damages. Defense counsel will notify Plaintiff in Pro Per (incarcerated litigant) of the Court's ruling. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
